codeReview.summary: The fix commit addresses the prior blocking gaps. Compared with the original failed commit (1a40485...), this change wires E2EE bootstrap into the real `mors init` runtime path (`src/init.ts`) and enforces bootstrap guards in real secure command paths (`send --secure` and `reply --secure` in `src/cli.ts`). Added tests cover init provisioning, cleanup/re-run behavior, and CLI guarded-failure/success paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true, but the feature changed user-facing CLI surfaces (`init`, `send --secure`, `reply --secure`) and the handoff/transcript evidence only lists automated commands with no manual CLI/API/SSE sanity check evidence or explicit N/A justification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md step 8 requires manual sanity checks for user-facing changes; handoff file 2026-02-28T12-27-29-184Z__relay-foundation-fix-e2ee-bootstrap-runtime-integration__ef0b98dc-4995-46b4-9d0f-99bfae2c745f.json lists only `verification.commandsRun`; transcript skeleton for session ef0b98dc-4995-46b4-9d0f-99bfae2c745f contains no manual/interactive check section.

summary: Pass. The fix adequately resolves the original failure by integrating device-key bootstrap into runtime init flow and adding actual command-path bootstrap guards for secure send/reply, with targeted and full-suite test evidence in handoff.
